description: The schema is denormalized and inefficient. Propose a normalized schema with migration queries.

scenarios.description: Denormalized order table, split into proper normalized tables
scenarios.schema_sql: CREATE TABLE all_orders (order_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, order_date TEXT, customer_name TEXT, customer_email TEXT, customer_phone TEXT, product_name TEXT, product_category TEXT, product_price REAL, quantity INTEGER, shipping_address TEXT, shipping_city TEXT, shipping_state TEXT);
scenarios.data_sql: INSERT INTO all_orders VALUES (1,'2024-01-01','Alice Smith','alice@example.com','555-0001','Laptop','Electronics',999.99,1,'123 Main St','Boston','MA'),(2,'2024-01-02','Alice Smith','alice@example.com','555-0001','Mouse','Electronics',29.99,2,'123 Main St','Boston','MA'),(3,'2024-01-03','Bob Jones','bob@example.com','555-0002','T-Shirt','Clothing',19.99,3,'456 Oak Ave','Chicago','IL'),(4,'2024-01-04','Alice Smith','alice@example.com','555-0001','Keyboard','Electronics',79.99,1,'123 Main St','Boston','MA');
problems: Customer data duplicated in every order row
Product data duplicated in every order row
Shipping address duplicated for same customer
No referential integrity, email can be inconsistent
Cannot query customers or products independently

normalized_schema_example.customers: CREATE TABLE customers (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, phone TEXT)
normalized_schema_example.products: CREATE TABLE products (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, name TEXT NOT NULL, category TEXT NOT NULL, price REAL NOT NULL)
normalized_schema_example.addresses: CREATE TABLE addresses (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, customer_id INTEGER REFERENCES customers(id), street TEXT, city TEXT, state TEXT)
normalized_schema_example.orders: CREATE TABLE orders (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, customer_id INTEGER REFERENCES customers(id), address_id INTEGER REFERENCES addresses(id), order_date TEXT NOT NULL)
normalized_schema_example.order_items: CREATE TABLE order_items (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, order_id INTEGER REFERENCES orders(id), product_id INTEGER REFERENCES products(id), quantity INTEGER NOT NULL, price_at_purchase REAL NOT NULL)
